insight:
Yesterday was a solid long run day! You crushed that 19.3km in 124 minutes with a controlled 148 bpm average heart rate, showing excellent aerobic fitness. Your 47 bpm resting HR confirms you're in great cardiovascular shape, and that low stress score of 34 indicates your body handled the training load well.

Your HRV of 108ms sits perfectly within your baseline range of 90-112ms, which is fantastic news. This means your nervous system recovered well overnight despite the big training session. You're green-lighted to stick with today's planned workout - no need to modify intensity or volume.

However, that 6-hour sleep after burning nearly 4,000 calories is concerning. You need at least 7-8 hours tonight to support recovery with the race just 14 days out. Consider an earlier bedtime and focus on getting 175g protein today to repair those hard-working muscles from yesterday's long effort.
